title: error loading cached network config: network "podman" not found in CNI cache
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)** /kind bug **Description** Failed to load rootfull container in ubuntu WSL2 with following error message: ```shell $ sudo podman run -dt --name webserver -p 8080:80 quay.io/libpod/banner ERRO[0000] error loading cached network config: network "podman" not found in CNI cache WARN[0000] falling back to loading from existing plugins on disk ERRO[0000] Error tearing down partially created network namespace for container 4efd9483fda0dbce0391dca8b2defce4c37fbfb5824d26b7ece89093d2cee126: error removing pod webserver_webserver from CNI network "podman": running [/usr/sbin/iptables -t nat -D POSTROUTING -s 10.88.2.3 -j CNI-38c2f017994ad2e35c286de0 -m comment --comment name: "podman" id: "4efd9483fda0dbce0391dca8b2defce4c37fbfb5824d26b7ece89093d2cee126" --wait]: exit status 2: iptables v1.8.7 (nf_tables): Couldn't load match `comment':No such file or directory Try `iptables -h' or 'iptables --help' for more information. Error: error configuring network namespace for container 4efd9483fda0dbce0391dca8b2defce4c37fbfb5824d26b7ece89093d2cee126: error adding pod webserver_webserver to CNI network "podman": running [/usr/sbin/iptables -t nat -C CNI-38c2f017994ad2e35c286de0 -d 10.88.2.3/24 -j ACCEPT -m comment --comment name: "podman" id: "4efd9483fda0dbce0391dca8b2defce4c37fbfb5824d26b7ece89093d2cee126" --wait]: exit status 2: iptables v1.8.7 (nf_tables): Couldn't load match `comment':No such file or directory Try `iptables -h' or 'iptables --help' for more information. $ sudo podman ps CONTAINER ID IMAGE COMMAND CREATED STATUS PORTS NAMES ``` **Steps to reproduce the issue:** 1. Upgrade to Ubuntu 22.04 and install podman: ```shell sudo apt-get -y install podman sudo usermod --add-subuids 100000-165535 --add-subgids 100000-165535 sudo chmod 4755 /usr/bin/newgidmap sudo chmod 4755 /usr/bin/newuidmap ``` 2. run a rootfull container ```shell sudo podman run -dt --name webserver -p 8080:80 quay.io/libpod/banner ``` **Describe the results you received:** ```shell ERRO[0000] error loading cached network config: network "podman" not found in CNI cache WARN[0000] falling back to loading from existing plugins on disk ERRO[0000] Error tearing down partially created network namespace for container 4efd9483fda0dbce0391dca8b2defce4c37fbfb5824d26b7ece89093d2cee126: error removing pod webserver_webserver from CNI network "podman": running [/usr/sbin/iptables -t nat -D POSTROUTING -s 10.88.2.3 -j CNI-38c2f017994ad2e35c286de0 -m comment --comment name: "podman" id: "4efd9483fda0dbce0391dca8b2defce4c37fbfb5824d26b7ece89093d2cee126" --wait]: exit status 2: iptables v1.8.7 (nf_tables): Couldn't load match `comment':No such file or directory Try `iptables -h' or 'iptables --help' for more information. Error: error configuring network namespace for container 4efd9483fda0dbce0391dca8b2defce4c37fbfb5824d26b7ece89093d2cee126: error adding pod webserver_webserver to CNI network "podman": running [/usr/sbin/iptables -t nat -C CNI-38c2f017994ad2e35c286de0 -d 10.88.2.3/24 -j ACCEPT -m comment --comment name: "podman" id: "4efd9483fda0dbce0391dca8b2defce4c37fbfb5824d26b7ece89093d2cee126" --wait]: exit status 2: iptables v1.8.7 (nf_tables): Couldn't load match `comment':No such file or directory Try `iptables -h' or 'iptables --help' for more information. ``` **Describe the results you expected:** ```shell $ docker ps CONTAINER ID IMAGE COMMAND CREATED STATUS PORTS NAMES XXXXXXXXXXXX quay.io/libpod/banner:latest nginx -g daemon o... 12 seconds ago Up 12 seconds ago 0.0.0.0:8080->80/tcp webserver $ curl localhost:8080 404 Not Found

404 Not Found

nginx
``` **Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):** - WSL2 Ubuntu 22.04 LTS version - Rootless command works: ```shell $ podman run -dt --name webserver -p 8080:80 quay.io/libpod/banner $ podman ps CONTAINER ID IMAGE COMMAND CREATED STATUS PORTS NAMES 8002d44668c1 quay.io/libpod/banner:latest nginx -g daemon o... 7 minutes ago Up 7 minutes ago 0.0.0.0:8080->80/tcp webserver $ curl localhost:8080 404 Not Found

404 Not Found

nginx
``` **Output of `podman version`:** ``` Version: 3.4.4 API Version: 3.4.4 Go Version: go1.17.3 Built: Thu Jan 1 07:30:00 1970 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 ``` **Output of `podman info --debug`:** ``` sudo podman info --debug host: arch: amd64 buildahVersion: 1.23.1 cgroupControllers: - cpuset - cpu - cpuacct - blkio - memory - devices - freezer - net_cls - perf_event - net_prio - hugetlb - pids - rdma cgroupManager: cgroupfs cgroupVersion: v1 conmon: package: 'conmon: /usr/bin/conmon' path: /usr/bin/conmon version: 'conmon version 2.0.25, commit: unknown' cpus: 4 distribution: codename: jammy distribution: ubuntu version: "22.04" eventLogger: file hostname: myPortege idMappings: gidmap: null uidmap: null kernel: 5.10.102.1-microsoft-standard-WSL2 linkmode: dynamic logDriver: journald memFree: 282423296 memTotal: 1505017856 ociRuntime: name: crun package: 'crun: /usr/bin/crun' path: /usr/bin/crun version: |- crun version 0.17 commit: 0e9229ae34caaebcb86f1fde18de3acaf18c6d9a spec: 1.0.0 +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL os: linux remoteSocket: exists: true path: /run/podman/podman.sock security: apparmorEnabled: false capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT rootless: false seccompEnabled: true seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json selinuxEnabled: false serviceIsRemote: false slirp4netns: executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns package: 'slirp4netns: /usr/bin/slirp4netns' version: |- slirp4netns version 1.0.1 commit: 6a7b16babc95b6a3056b33fb45b74a6f62262dd4 libslirp: 4.6.1 swapFree: 524288000 swapTotal: 524288000 uptime: 43m 34.14s plugins: log: - k8s-file - none - journald network: - bridge - macvlan volume: - local registries: {} store: configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf containerStore: number: 0 paused: 0 running: 0 stopped: 0 graphDriverName: overlay graphOptions: {} graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage graphStatus: Backing Filesystem: extfs Native Overlay Diff: "true" Supports d_type: "true" Using metacopy: "false" imageStore: number: 1 runRoot: /run/containers/storage volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes version: APIVersion: 3.4.4 Built: 0 BuiltTime: Thu Jan 1 07:30:00 1970 GitCommit: "" GoVersion: go1.17.3 OsArch: linux/amd64 Version: 3.4.4 ``` **Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):** ```shell $ apt list podman Listing... Done podman/jammy,now 3.4.4+ds1-1ubuntu1 amd64 [installed] ``` **Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)** Yes (3.4.4 is the latest version in the ubuntu apt package manager) **Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):** - Windows 10 laptop ``` Microsoft Windows Version 21H2 (OS Build 19044.1645) ``` - WSL2 Ubuntu 22.04 LTS version ``` Distributor ID: Ubuntu Description: Ubuntu 22.04 LTS Release: 22.04 Codename: jammy ```

comments.body:
I don't think this is a podman issue. I guess the iptables/nftables kernel modules are not loaded correctly, see https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/7948

Does it work on a normal ubuntu install? If so I think this is a problem with the WSL kernel.

@Luap99 If, as @davdr suggested there,  you have to update iptables on the os to use the legacy mode,  I would think that the issue is with incomplete Podman implementation.  

One shouldn't downgrade the OS because an incomplete package won't work. In most cases you wouldn't allowed to do that in your deployment platform. Even if you are allowed, you wouldn't know the collateral impact to the rest of other application in that system. 

Running Ubuntu under WS2 is perfectly valid scenario that more and more developers are doing. Note that docker for windows are perfectly working in this scenario. I can run docker-in-docker without any struggle. I don't have a normal ubuntu install on my laptop since ages, so i can't answer that question. I assume it will work otherwise no one will use podman. 

Since podman team refused to work on this issue, should I just give up and conclude that Podman can not replace docker as it is missing essential feature of running priviledge container in WSL2 scenario?

Note that this is required by podman itself to be able to run podman inside a podman container and to expose the containers, ip to the host.

Podman team should make it clear in the documentation that running podman under WSL2 is severely limited, to avoid people wasting time trying to use this product.

Try to run `iptables -A INPUT -i <INTERFACE> -m comment --comment "test comment"` and check if this works

Podman works with iptables-nft just fine (at least this is used on fedora and RHEL). If this does not work on debian/ubuntu it is a bug on that distro with that iptables or kernel version and not podman. There is nothing we can do to fix this.

FWIW, Docker CE has exactly the same problem under WSL2: https://dev.to/lemosluan/comment/1nmi7
Looks like the WSL2 kernel is not compiled with the necessary flags for nftables to work.

> iptables -A INPUT -i <INTERFACE> -m comment --comment "test comment"

@Luap99, it doesn't seem to work. 

```shell
$ iptables -A INPUT -i eth0 -m comment --comment "test comment"

iptables v1.8.7 (nf_tables): Couldn't load match `comment':No such file or directory

Try `iptables -h' or 'iptables --help' for more information.
```

> FWIW, Docker CE has exactly the same problem under WSL2: https://dev.to/lemosluan/comment/1nmi7 Looks like the WSL2 kernel is not compiled with the necessary flags for nftables to work. @davdr, Docker CE works in WSL2. ```shell $ sudo docker run -dt --privileged --name webserver3 -p 8083:80 quay.io/libpod/banner 6e94ccfbbe546b87866ea654ecdb999c00164d6e79c6e4112383c14092402db2 $ docker ps -a CONTAINER ID IMAGE COMMAND CREATED STATUS PORTS NAMES 6e94ccfbbe54 quay.io/libpod/banner "nginx -g 'daemon of…" 14 seconds ago Up 11 seconds 0.0.0.0:8083->80/tcp webserver3 $ curl localhost:8083 404 Not Found

404 Not Found

nginx
$ docker version Client: Docker Engine - Community Cloud integration: v1.0.24 Version: 20.10.14 API version: 1.41 Go version: go1.16.15 Git commit: a224086 Built: Thu Mar 24 01:48:21 2022 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 Context: default Experimental: true Server: Docker Desktop Engine: Version: 20.10.14 API version: 1.41 (minimum version 1.12) Go version: go1.16.15 Git commit: 87a90dc Built: Thu Mar 24 01:46:14 2022 OS/Arch: linux/amd64 Experimental: false containerd: Version: 1.5.11 GitCommit: 3df54a852345ae127d1fa3092b95168e4a88e2f8 runc: Version: 1.0.3 GitCommit: v1.0.3-0-gf46b6ba docker-init: Version: 0.19.0 GitCommit: de40ad0 ```

@awidjaja Looks like that's the Docker CE client talking to the Docker Desktop daemon, which is probably running in another WSL 2 distro managed by Docker Desktop and that doesn't use nftables either (because all WSL 2 distros share the same WSL 2 kernel that doesn't support nftables).

@davdr, thx for the explanation, but that is actually my request. Can't podman, who position itself as docker alternative, study how that docker daemon WSL2 distro able to make this work without the nftables and at least provide a configuration option to make podman work in WSL2 distro? Why closing this issue without due consideration as if this isn't a legitimate need?
This issue still occurs on 3.4.4 on Ubuntu WSL

Today upgraded my Ubuntu to 22.04 under WSL2 and installed podman-compose. Trying to run some examples from podman-compose and getting every time the error:
ERRO[0000] error loading cached network config: network "<directoryname>_default" not found in CNI cache

I was able to workaround this issue with: 

`sudo update-alternatives --set iptables /usr/sbin/iptables-legacy`

[From here](https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/7948#issuecomment-1207210311). On Windows 10, Ubuntu WSL 22.04 with Podman 3.4.4.
